title: Avatar: negative review
body: Last Friday after lunch, invited by Adobe, I went to the 3D Avatar premiere at Cinesa Diagonal in Barcelona. As a requirement and fair payment for the invitation, we listened to the company's Software representative in Spain, a large bucket of popcorn and a Coca-Cola, also courtesy of Adobe, made the talk very pleasant in which he told us about the virtues of his products, programs that have intervened in the editing of the film although not in its 3D modeling phase. Without the usual advertisements and trailers in movie theaters and almost without giving me time to put on the glasses necessary for the 3D experience, James Cameron's latest film began. I have to say that the film was entertaining, but nothing more. It has actually been one of the worst things I've seen at the movies lately, if I had paid for the ticket I would have felt bad about wasting that money and that time, almost 3 hours. For me Avatar is one of those unpretentious movies to watch at home on a Sunday afternoon. My surprise was afterwards hearing the comments from the spectators, generally positive although not very effusive. I also wanted to follow [what was being said on Twitter](http://twitter.com/#search?q=avatar%203d), but since it was a premiere at those hours people were only talking about expectations and how eager they were to see it. Today, Sunday, I wanted to look at reviews again and I continue to be surprised because I haven't found any negative opinions, I felt like a stranger bug compared to those that appear in the film. That's why I want to give my opinion about Avatar, this blockbuster that mixes real images with digital ones and that takes advantage of the latest technology to be able to watch it in the 3 dimensions that reality gives us. Technically it hasn't seemed like any marvel to me, 3D is spectacular, but it still doesn't achieve a real perception of this three-dimensionality (you are not the one who decides which part of the image you want to focus on). In my opinion, a film is good if it has a good script, a good story and if the actors make it credible. **Avatar is not original at all**, they just insist on ideas used already 30 or 40 years ago, it is clearly inspired by successful sagas like Star Wars, Alien or Matrix, it doesn't add anything new besides having **a completely incoherent and predictable script**. 3D, special effects and digital animation do not justify by themselves the success of a film. Avatar surely has its audience but I think the evolution of cinema is something else, **calling this film a revolution and a before and after in the history of the seventh art is totally exaggerated**. And deep down I feel sorry, because **once more the opportunity to change and realize that interesting, beautiful and entertaining things can be done without abusing action and violence has been wasted**. What do you think?
